title: Dark Roasted Jamaican Date Stew
description: A hearty, chewy, and nostalgic plant-based stew featuring roasted stoned dates, cooked to perfection in a ttukbaegi earthenware pot. This Jamaican-inspired dish is perfect for a chilly evening.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a ttukbaegi earthenware pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and saute until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for an additional minute.
Add the ground allspice and dried thyme and stir to combine.
Add the pitted dates, vegetable broth, and coconut milk, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Roast the stew for 30 minutes, or until the dates are tender and the liquid has thickened.
If using scotch bonnet peppers, stir them in during the last 10 minutes of cooking.
Remove the pot from the oven and let the stew cool slightly before serving.
